{{Short description|none}} {{EngvarB|date=April 2022}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2022}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 2022 Cannes Film Festival | image = 2022 Cannes Film Festival.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 75th Cannes Film Festival featuring a homage to ''[[The Truman Show]]'' (1998) | opening = ''[[Final Cut (2022 film)|Final Cut]]'' | closing = | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Triangle of Sadness]]'' | host = [[Virginie Efira]] | number = 21 (In Competition) | date = 17–28 May 2022 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2021 Cannes Film Festival|2021]] | next = [[2023 Cannes Film Festival|2023]] }} The '''75th annual [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' is a film festival that took place from 17 to 28 May 2022.{{cite web |title=The 75th edition will take place from May 17 to 28, 2022. Registration of films is open! |url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/info/articles/the-75th-edition-will-take-place-from-may-17-to-28-2022-registration-of-films-is-open |website=[[Cannes Film Festival]] |access-date=5 April 2022 |date=30 November 2021 |archive-date=3 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220503191646/https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/infos-communiques/info/articles/the-75th-edition-will-take-place-from-may-17-to-28-2022-registration-of-films-is-open |url-status=live }} French actor [[Vincent Lindon]] served as jury president for the main competition. French actress [[Virginie Efira]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies. Swedish filmmaker [[Ruben Östlund]] won the ''[[Palme d'Or]]'', the festival's top prize, for a second time with the comedy-drama film ''[[Triangle of Sadness]].'' The [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] was awarded to American actors [[Forest Whitaker]] and [[Tom Cruise]], the latter on short notice during the international premiere of ''[[Top Gun: Maverick]]''.{{cite web |last1=Fleming |first1=Mike Jr. |title=Tom Cruise To Be Feted In Career Retrospective At Cannes, Followed By ''Top Gun: Maverick'' Premiere; Full Details |url=https://deadline.com/2022/03/tom-cruise-cannes-film-festival-career-retrospective-may-18-top-gun-maverick-premiere-1234980786/ |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |access-date=5 April 2022 |date=17 March 2022 |archive-date=30 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220530224952/https://deadline.com/2022/03/tom-cruise-cannes-film-festival-career-retrospective-may-18-top-gun-maverick-premiere-1234980786/ |url-status=live }}{{Cite web |last1=Brzeski |first1=Patrick |last2=Ritman |first2=Alex |date=18 May 2022 |title=Tom Cruise and 'Top Gun: Maverick' Touch Down in Cannes With Fighter Jets, Surprise Palme d'Or |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/tom-cruise-cannes-top-gun-premiere-1235149411/ |access-date=19 May 2022 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |language=en-US |archive-date=20 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220520095603/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/tom-cruise-cannes-top-gun-premiere-1235149411/ |url-status=live }} The official poster for the festival was designed as a homage to ''[[The Truman Show]]'' (1998), the film was also selected for the ''Cinéma de la Plage'' section.{{cite web |url=https://www.vogue.fr/fashion-culture/article/2022-cannes-festival-jury |title=Cannes Film Festival 2022: The official poster pays tribute to the cult film The Truman Show |work=Vogue |date=21 March 2022 |access-date=24 April 2022 |archive-date=3 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220503010809/https://www.vogue.fr/fashion-culture/article/2022-cannes-festival-jury |url-status=live }} The festival opened with ''[[Final Cut (2022 film)|Final Cut]]'' by [[Michel Hazanavicius]]. The edition also marked the festival's return to its full spectator capacity after the disruption in its previous two year's editions due to [[COVID-19 pandemic in France|COVID-19 restrictions in Short Film Competition== The 2022 Cannes Film Festival also saw the first edition of the ''TikTok Short Film Competition''. It was organized in partnership with social media giant [[TikTok]], and was part of a desire to diversify the audience of the festival.[https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/23/movies/cannes-tiktok.html Cannes Tiktok] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221122000302/https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/23/movies/cannes-tiktok.html |date=22 November 2022 }} The New York Times (subscription required) The jury consisted of Oscar-nominated director [[Rithy Panh]],[https://www.wsj.com/articles/BL-SJB-13619 Rithy Panh Looks for 'The Missing Picture'] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221126142438/https://www.wsj.com/articles/BL-SJB-13619 |date=26 November 2022 }} The Washington Post world-famous TikTok creator [[Khaby Lame]], director Camille Ducellier, director-stylist Basma Khalifa and writer-director Angele Diabang.{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/|title=Variety|website=Variety|accessdate=18 December 2022|archive-date=17 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140217020107/http://variety.com/|url-status=live}} The first Grand Prix Award for Best Film was given to Matej Rimanić, for his film ''[[Love In Plane Sight]]'', the second Grand Prix Award was given to Mabuta Motoki, for his film ''Is it Okay to Chop Down Trees?''. The jury also awarded Tim Hamilton with his film ''Zero Gravity'' for Best Edit, and Claudia Cochet with her film ''Modern Princess'' for Best Script.[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/tik-tok-cannes-short-film-awards-winners-1235151180/ Cannes: TikTok’s Short-Film Competition Awards Two Grand Prix After Jury Controversy] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221122000304/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/tik-tok-cannes-short-film-awards-winners-1235151180/ |date=22 November 2022 }} The Hollywood Reporter. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{Official website|www.festival-cannes.com/en.html}} {{Cannes Film Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Cannes Film Festival}} [[Category:2022 film festivals]] [[Category:Cannes Film Festival|2022]] [[Category:2022 festivals in Europe]] [[Category:2022 in French cinema]] [[Category:May 2022 in France]]
